visa_free.notes: EU/EEA citizens do not need a visa or residence permit to enter Slovenia. They require only a valid identity card or passport. Freedom of movement applies.

UK nationals (British citizens) are visa-free for Schengen area including Slovenia for up to 90 days in any 180-day period following Brexit, under the post-Brexit arrangement.

visa_policy.notes: Slovenia is a Schengen Area member state and applies EU Regulation 2018/1806 uniformly. Visa-free list is Annex II of that Regulation. VANUATU CORRECTION (validated June 2026): Vanuatu nationals are NO LONGER visa-free. EU Regulation 2025/11 (adopted 19 December 2024, effective 3 February 2025) permanently transferred Vanuatu from Annex II to Annex I (visa required). Annex I (visa required) now includes Vanuatu in addition to: Afghanistan, Armenia, Angola, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Burkina Faso, Bahrain, Burundi, Benin, Bolivia, Bhutan, Botswana, Belarus, Belize, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Central African Republic, Congo, Cote d'Ivoire, Cameroon, China, Cuba, Cape Verde, Djibouti, Dominican Republic, Algeria, Ecuador, Egypt, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Fiji, Gabon, Ghana, The Gambia, Guinea, Equatorial Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Guyana, Haiti, Indonesia, India, Iraq, Iran, Jamaica, Jordan, Kenya, Kyrgyzstan, Cambodia, Comoros, North Korea, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Laos, Lebanon, Sri Lanka, Liberia, Lesotho, Libya, Morocco, Madagascar, Mali, Myanmar/Burma, Mongolia, Mauritania, Maldives, Malawi, Mozambique, Namibia, Niger, Nigeria, Nepal, Oman, Papua New Guinea, Philippines, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia (suspended), Rwanda, Saudi Arabia, Sudan, Sierra Leone, Senegal, Somalia, Suriname, South Sudan, Sao Tome and Principe, Syria, Chad, Togo, Thailand, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Tunisia, Turkey, Tanzania, Uganda, Uzbekistan, Vietnam, Yemen, South Africa, Zambia, Zimbabwe, Palestinian Authority. ETIAS NOTE: As of June 2026 ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) has not yet launched; it is scheduled for Q4 2026. When launched, visa-exempt nationals (e.g. USA, CAN, AUS, JPN, GBR) will need to obtain an ETIAS pre-travel authorisation (EUR 20, 3-year validity) before arriving in Slovenia. ENTRY/EXIT SYSTEM: EES began operations on 12 October 2025. Slovenia has no national e-visa system separate from the Schengen framework. Short-stay Schengen visa costs EUR 90 for adults, EUR 45 for children aged 6-12; processed normally within 15 days (up to 45 days).

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a short-stay visa (Type C) issued by any Schengen country may, during the visa validity period, enter the Republic of Slovenia and stay in its territory, provided that the total length of their stay in Schengen countries does not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period, or until the expiry of the visa, whichever is sooner. Applies to nationalities that would otherwise require a visa for Slovenia (Annex I countries).

Holders of a long-stay visa (Type D) issued by any Schengen country may enter the Republic of Slovenia with this visa and a valid travel document and stay in its territory, provided that the total length of their stay in Schengen countries, except in the territory of the Schengen country that issued the long-stay visa, does not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period, or until the expiry of the long-stay visa, whichever is sooner. Applies to nationalities that would otherwise require a visa for Slovenia.

Holders of a residence permit issued by any Schengen country may enter the Republic of Slovenia with this residence permit and a valid travel document and stay in its territory, provided that the total length of their stay in Schengen countries, except in the territory of the Schengen country that issued the residence permit, does not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period, or until the expiry of the residence permit, whichever is sooner. Applies to nationalities that would otherwise require a visa for Slovenia.

Holders of a valid Chinese diplomatic passport are visa-free for short stays in Slovenia (and all Schengen area states) under the EU-China short-stay visa waiver agreement for holders of diplomatic passports. Maximum 90 days in any 180-day period. Agreement entered into force 1 January 2017.

Holders of Brazilian diplomatic, service or official passports are visa-free for short stays in Slovenia (and all Schengen area states) under the EU-Brazil short-stay visa waiver agreement for holders of diplomatic, service or official passports. Agreement entered into force 1 April 2011; amended agreement published OJ L 179, 14.7.2023.

Holders of Mongolian diplomatic and service passports are visa-free for Slovenia under a bilateral visa waiver agreement signed between Slovenia and Mongolia on 15 October 2020, concluded under EU Regulation allowing Member States to define exemptions from visa requirements for holders of diplomatic, service and other status passports. Specific stay duration not published in the official announcement.

Holders of Indian diplomatic passports do not need a short-stay visa to enter Slovenia. Ordinary Indian passport holders (including OCI cardholders) require a Schengen visa. Source: official gov.si country page for India.

cbi.notes: Slovenia does not offer a citizenship-by-investment program. No legal framework exists to obtain Slovenian citizenship solely through investment. Citizenship is available only through naturalisation after 10 years of total legal residence (with the last 5 years continuous). Confirmed by absence of any official program on gov.si or mnz.gov.si.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit for Business Investors / Company Director
programs.notes: Non-EU nationals may obtain a work permit (and thus residence) by establishing or investing in a Slovenian limited liability company (d.o.o.). For companies not yet registered for 6 months, proof of investment of at least EUR 30,000 for company start-up is required to obtain a work permit for a foreign representative. The permit is issued for up to 2 years and is renewable. After 5 years of continuous legal residence, permanent residency is available; citizenship via naturalisation after 10 years total. There is no dedicated 'golden visa' or standalone investor visa program — residence flows from business activity, not passive investment.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit for Digital Nomads
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA nationals who work remotely for a company or clients registered outside Slovenia, or are self-employed abroad. Must have monthly funds of at least twice the average monthly net salary in Slovenia (approximately EUR 3,200/month as of early 2026). Must hold valid health insurance (min EUR 30,000 coverage) and have a clean criminal record.
programs.notes: Launched 21 November 2025. Permit issued for up to one year; cannot be extended. Reapplication possible 6 months after expiry. Alternative path: holders may switch to other residence permit categories at any time during permit validity. Family members may join immediately without duration restrictions. Applications submitted at Slovenian diplomatic missions abroad or at any administrative unit in Slovenia.

programs.name: EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with a valid work contract or binding job offer for highly qualified employment (at least 6 months under 2025 amendments) paying at least 1.5x the average annual gross salary in Slovenia. Requires higher professional qualifications (university level) or, for IT professionals, proven competence via certificates or expert opinion.
programs.processing_time: 30 days (up to 60 days for complex cases)
programs.notes: Issued for the duration of the employment contract plus 3 months, maximum 2 years; renewable for up to 3 years. Labour market test applies. Initial application fee EUR 70-102; residence permit card EUR 15.47. 2025 amendments loosened requirements: contract minimum reduced from 1 year to 6 months, and IT professionals may qualify without academic degrees.

programs.name: Single Permit (Combined Residence and Work Permit)
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals seeking employment in Slovenia. Employer must demonstrate no suitable EU/Slovenian worker was available (labour market test).
programs.notes: Combines residence and work permit into a single administrative procedure. Available for various employment categories including seasonal workers and intra-corporate transferees.

programs.name: Work Permit for Foreign Company Representative / Director
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals who are directors or procurators of a Slovenian company (d.o.o. or other entity). Companies with 10 or fewer workers may obtain no more than one such permit.
programs.notes: For companies registered less than 6 months: minimum investment of EUR 30,000 for company start-up required. Valid for up to 2 years; renewable. Application fee approximately EUR 90.

validation_notes: CORRECTION (June 2026 re-validation): Vanuatu (VUT) removed from visa_policy.visa_free. EU Regulation 2025/11 (adopted 19 December 2024) permanently transferred Vanuatu from Annex II to Annex I effective 3 February 2025. Prior record incorrectly listed Vanuatu as visa-free with only a suspension note. Vanuatu nationals now require a Schengen visa to enter Slovenia. Source: consilium.europa.eu press release 12 Dec 2024; nyidanmark.dk (Danish immigration authority confirming permanent status); EUR-Lex OJ L 202402059.

CORRECTION (June 2026 re-validation): Kosovo source_url updated from the 'signing' announcement (April 2023) to the 'entry into force' announcement (January 2024) confirming the effective date of 1 January 2024. Source: home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/visa-free-travel-kosovo-citizens-eu-2024-01-03_en.
ADDITION (June 2026 re-validation): visa_policy.notes updated with ETIAS status (not yet launched; planned Q4 2026) and EES (launched 12 October 2025) for completeness.

CLARIFICATION (June 2026 re-validation): Digital Nomad permit income threshold of EUR 3,200/month confirmed by multiple secondary sources consistent with the official gov.si formulation of 'twice the average monthly net salary'. The official_url confirmed as live and accurate.

No change to conditional_access entries: Schengen visa/RP credential-based access, Chinese diplomatic passport waiver, Brazilian diplomatic/service/official passport waiver, Mongolian diplomatic/service bilateral waiver, Indian diplomatic passport exemption — all remain as prior record.
gaps: ETIAS exact launch date within Q4 2026 not yet officially announced as of June 2026.

Mongolia bilateral diplomatic/service passport waiver (signed 15 Oct 2020) — exact entry-into-force date and permitted stay duration not published in the official gov.si news release; only the signing date is confirmed.

Comprehensive list of all bilateral diplomatic/service passport visa waiver agreements signed by Slovenia could not be retrieved from mzz.gov.si (connection errors during validation). Only Mongolia is confirmed via an official gov.si news release; India diplomatic passport exemption confirmed via gov.si/en/countries/india page. Additional bilateral agreements may exist but are not verifiable from accessible official sources.
Digital Nomad permit exact processing time not stated in official gov.si source.

The official Slovenia MFA website (mzz.gov.si) was not directly accessible during re-validation (connection errors); visa policy data was sourced from the authoritative EU Regulation 2018/1806 (official EU source) which uniformly governs all Schengen states including Slovenia.

Annex II note: some countries (e.g. Dominica, Grenada, FSM, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Nauru, Palau, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Timor-Leste, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu-removed) were listed with pending bilateral visa waiver agreement conditions; their exemption status depends on whether the bilateral EU agreement has entered into force.
No official Slovenian-specific e-visa portal found; Slovenia applies standard Schengen visa procedures with no national e-visa scheme.

UK nationals' post-Brexit 90-day visa-free status for Schengen is referenced from the gov.si entry page; the specific mechanism (UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement) was not separately verified at source during this validation cycle.

outbound_official.note: No official Slovenia government outbound visa-list page located. Slovenia's Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (mzz.gov.si) and the e-government portal (e-uprava.gov.si) do not publish a consolidated list of countries where Slovenian ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. Official Slovenian government pages (e-uprava.gov.si/si/podrocja/potovanja-vizumi/dokumenti-vizumi/vizumski-postopki.html and e-uprava.gov.si/en/podrocja/travel/documents-and-visas/visa-procedures.html) direct citizens to consult individual embassy and consulate pages via the 'Predstavništva po svetu' (Representations Worldwide) directory at mzz.gov.si/si/predstavnistva_po_svetu/ — a per-embassy contact directory, not a compiled visa-free country list. The mzz.gov.si domain was unresponsive to direct fetch attempts. No origin-side official page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin was found. Rely on destination-side data.
